original: A possible alien radio signal from deep space, later dubbed the "Wow!" signal, is received at Big Ear, Ohio State's radio telescope in Delaware

The Wizard of Oz, an American musical fantasy film adaptation of L. Frank Baum's book, premieres at Grauman's Chinese Theatre in Hollywood; directed by Victor Fleming and King Vidor with a cast featuring Judy Garland (Dorothy), Ray Bolger (Scarecrow), Jack Haley (Tin Man), Bert Lahr (Cowardly Lion), Frank Morgan (Wizard), Billie Burke (Glinda), andMargaret Hamilton(Wicked Witch); songs by Harold Arlen and Yip Harburg
Tivoli Gardens, the world's second-oldest amusement park, opens in Copenhagen, Denmark
Panama Canal opens under budget with the SS Ancon making the first official steamship passage through the canal
The Mayflower sets sail from Southampton, England, with 102 Pilgrims bound for the New World
Julia Child, American chef, author and television personality (The French Chef), born in Pasadena, California (d. 2004)
Woodstock Festival opens in Bethel, New York, on Max Yasgur's dairy farm; performers include Richie Havens, Tim Hardin,Ravi Shankar, Melanie, Arlo Guthrie, and Joan Baez
"Macarena" single is released by Los del Río
Patricia Palinkas becomes the first woman professional football player (Orlando)
USAF Captain Joe B. Jordan reaches 31,513 meters in an F-104 jet fighter
David Simons reaches 30,942 meters in the Manhigh II balloon
A UFO is sighted by three California patrolmen
US PresidentRonald Reagandecides to support a replacement for Challenger
The Beatles play to their largest crowd of over 55,000 at Shea Stadium, New York City; among those attending are future wivesBarbara BachandLinda Eastman
Yankee Guy Zinn sets a record by stealing home twice in a game
First recorded hurricane in North America hits the Plymouth Colony
Ageeda Paavel, Estonian freedom fighter who blew up a Soviet war monument at age 15, born in Tallinn, Republic of Estonia (d. 2023)
Richard Grant and Ernest Lassy complete the longest canoe journey without portages or any aid (6,102 miles; 9,820 km)
Victory over Japan Day, surrender of Japan, signifies the end of World War II (August 14 in the Americas due to the time zone difference)
"Mainz Psalter," the earliest dated book and the first to be printed in three colors, is completed
Beverly Burns, American pilot and 1st woman to captain a Boeing 747, born in Baltimore, Maryland
Edith Nesbit, British children books author (The Story of the Treasure Seekers, Five Children and It), born in London, England (d. 1924)
South African PresidentNelson Mandelareceives the Anne Frank Penning
India gains independence from the United Kingdom and remains a dominion until 1950
World War II: Korean Liberation Day
First freight delivery tunnel system begins operation underneath Chicago
"The Sinking of the Lusitania" by Winsor McCay is produced; at 12 minutes long, it is the longest work of animation at that time
GLEEP (Graphite Low Energy Experimental Pile), the first experimental nuclear reactor in Western Europe, begins operation in Oxford, England
Arch Rock, a danger to San Francisco Bay shipping, is blasted with 30 tons of nitroglycerin
Louis Victor de Broglie, French quantum physicist (studied electrons, Nobel Prize for Physics, 1929), born in Dieppe, France (d. 1987)
Robert L. Forward, American physicist and sci-fi writer (Dragon's Egg, Starquake), born in Geneva, NY (d. 2002)
Tony Robinson, British author, comedian and actor (Black Adder, Maid Marian and Her Merry Men), born in Homerton, London
Bill Baird, American puppeteer (Kukla Fran & Ollie, Muppet Show), born in Grand Island, Nebraska (d. 1987)
Jimmy Webb, American singer and songwriter ("MacArthur Park"; "Up Up & Away"), born in Elk City, Oklahoma
Tom Johnston, American guitarist and vocalist (The Doobie Brothers, 1970-77 & 1987-present - "Listen To The Music"; "China Grove"), born in Visalia, California
Larry Hartsell, American martial artist, bodyguard, trainer, student of Bruce Lee and Dan Inosanto, born in Charlotte, North Carolina (d. 2007)
Japanese community of San Francisco holdsMasanori MurakamiDay at Candlestick Park to honor the first Japanese player to play in the major leagues
At the Pan Am Games in Indianapolis, USA and Cuba are tied with 2 outs in the 9th inning when Ty Griffin hits a home run, resulting in Cuba's first loss in 20 years of Pan Am competition
MLB Philadelphia Phillies turn a triple play in an 8-1 victory over the Mets at Shea Stadium, New York City
Italian swimmer Giorgio Lamberti sets a world record in the 200 m freestyle (1:46.69) in Bonn, West Germany
New Zealand's All Blacks play their first rugby test match against Australia's Wallabies at the Sydney Cricket Ground, the All Blacks win 22-3
Mayor Richard J. Daley declares "Ernie BanksDay" in Chicago, honoring the Cubs baseball player
Indian action-adventure film "Sholay," directed by Ramesh Sippy and starring Dharmendra andAmitabh Bachchan, is released as the highest-grossing Indian film ever
Allies land on Kiska, Aleutian Islands
Operation Dragoon: Allied troops land in Provence
Transcontinental Railway is actually completed in Colorado
San Sebastian Church in Manila, the first all-steel church in Asia, is officially inaugurated and blessed
Annibale Frossi scores twice as Italy beats Austria 2-1 in extra time in the football final at the Berlin Olympics
Fred Trueman takes his 300th Test cricket wicket (Neil Hawke)
Puerto Rican champion José Torres defeats American Eddie Cotton on points in Las Vegas to retain the WBC/WBA light-heavyweight boxing title

Despite crashing out on lap 36, ScotsmanJackie Stewartclinches his second Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship at the Austrian Grand Prix at Österreichring; Swiss driver Jo Siffert of the BRM team wins the race
19th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Los Angeles 10, All-Stars 7 (88,316 attendees)
Charles Lismont wins the Helsinki marathon in 2:13:09.00
Kenneth Carr, American basketball forward (Olympic gold 1976; LA Lakers, Cleveland Cavaliers, Detroit Pistons, Portland TBs), born in Clearwater, Florida
Nesbert "Stix" Hooper, American jazz drummer (The Crusaders), born in Houston, Texas
Manley Lanier "Sonny" Carter Jr, American chemist and NASA astronaut (STS 33), born in Macon, Georgia (d. 1991)
Larry Mathews [Mazzeo], American child actor (The Dick Van Dyke Show - "Ritchie"), born in Burbank, California
Richard F. Heck, American chemist who discovered the Heck Reaction (Nobel Prize 2010), born in Springfield, Massachusetts (d. 2015)
Leslie Comrie, New Zealand astronomer and computing pioneer, born in Pukekohe, New Zealand (d. 1950)
Samuel Coleridge-Taylor, African-British composer (Hiawatha's Wedding Feast), born in London (d. 1912)
